Week 1: ┬╖ HTTP/HTTPS Model + Dev Environment
Week 1: ┬╖ Client/Server Concepts + REST Design
Week 1: ┬╖ DNS тАФ From Browser to IP
Week 1: ┬╖ How Websites Work тАФ Browser Rendering Pipeline
Week 1: ┬╖ CLI + Shell Scripting

Week 2: ┬╖ Flexbox + Grid + Responsive Design

days.title: Weekend
tasks:
All 5 platform landing pages: semantic HTML, Tailwind, Shadcn, Lighthouse 90+, deployed to Cloudflare Pages. WAL implementation started тАФ WAL writer + recovery on startup by end of weekend.
## Weeks 3тАУ4 тАФ JavaScript Engine: Types, Scope, Closures, Prototypes, Event Loop, TypeScript
*(Coverage identical to original plan тАФ all JavaScript internals, closures, prototypes, event loop phases, async/await, generators, TypeScript strict mode, Zod, branded types. Applied to OpenTrace utilities: `packages/utils` contains retry, emitter, scheduler, ConcurrencyLimiter. `packages/types` contains all branded entity IDs. `packages/schemas` contains Zod schemas for OTLP span validation.)*

days.title: Weekend
tasks: All 5 platforms have raw HTTP servers. WAL KV store: WAL writer, recovery, log compaction, benchmark results in `BENCHMARKS.md`. `goleak.VerifyNone` passing.

weeks.title: Overview

days.title: Weekend
tasks: OpenTrace Collector deployed and load tested. A small Go test harness generates synthetic OTLP spans to verify the Collector end-to-end тАФ spans arrive over gRPC, are validated, and land in the Kafka topic. PayCore (in its own repo) has auth and webhook design complete this month independently. TCP connection pool: pgwire protocol parsing, transaction mode and session mode, benchmark vs raw PostgreSQL. ADR written: "Why PgBouncer transaction mode over session mode."

weeks.title: Overview

days.title: Weekend
tasks: `go test -race ./...` passes everywhere. `goleak.VerifyNone` passes everywhere. Pipeline Processor deployed тАФ the synthetic span generator from Month 2 now flows through Kafka into ClickHouse, proving the full Collector тЖТ Kafka тЖТ Processor тЖТ ClickHouse pipeline works end-to-end. DungBeetle (in its own repo) has its v0.1 Go job queue running. Clustered WebSocket chat server: Redis pub/sub fan-out, distributed presence, sub-10ms delivery benchmark.

weeks.title: Overview

days.title: Weekend
tasks: All 5 projects have Next.js/React frontends, Go backends, PostgreSQL + Redis, JWT auth, full test suites тАФ each in its own GitHub repo with its own CI pipeline. OpenTrace UI v0.1: trace list, trace detail, basic waterfall renders real spans from the synthetic span generator. DNS Resolver: cold/warm cache benchmark. Notification Delivery Service: its own repo, multi-channel delivery, reliable queue, idempotency, webhook receipts тАФ consumed independently by RouteMaster and PayCore via a clean REST API.

Week 15: ┬╖ Kafka Essentials

tasks: All 5 platforms using correct database choices. ClickHouse Storage Layer deployed: `EXPLAIN` shows partition pruning working. `pg_stat_statements` catching slow queries. PgBouncer in transaction mode. URL Shortener + Rate Limiter deployed. Biweekly Project 7 (Distributed Lock Service) started.

weeks.title: Week 17: B тАФ Full-Stack Observability
Week 18: тАФ PayCore + Financial Systems + System Design

days.title: Weekend
tasks: All 5 projects deployed to ECS Fargate тАФ each from its own repo, its own Terraform config, its own CI pipeline. OpenTrace Query Service + API Gateway deployed; the self-referential trace demo is live. Each of the other four projects has its own Prometheus + Grafana stack and emits OTel traces to its own local Jaeger instance (not to OpenTrace тАФ they are independent). PITR drill executed on PayCore's PostgreSQL. Biweekly Project 7 (Distributed Lock Service) complete тАФ BookWise uses it internally for seat reservation locking.

title: 7: DISTRIBUTED SYSTEMS DEEP + BLOB/CDN/EDGE + SYSTEM DESIGN PART 3

weeks.title: Overview

days.title: Weekend
tasks: OpenTrace UI v2: service map renders the real self-referential dependency graph (UI тЖТ API Gateway тЖТ Query Service тЖТ ClickHouse). Live tail WebSocket streams new incoming spans in real time. SDK: a standalone sample app demonstrates zero-code-change auto-instrumentation тАФ one import, spans flowing. LSM-tree: 1M write benchmark, compaction strategy, Bloom filter shortcutting reads, documented in `BENCHMARKS.md`. DungBeetle v3.0 (its own repo): Kafka job queue, leader election, exactly-once cron тАФ fully independent milestone. BookWise waitlist engine (its own repo): Kafka event-driven state machine for waitlist assignment.

title: 8: AI-NATIVE STACK + PERFORMANCE ENGINEERING + FINAL SYSTEM DESIGNS

weeks.title: Overview

days.title: Weekend
tasks: K8s Operator for OpenTrace deployed. OTel compatibility verified: the official `go.opentelemetry.io/otel` SDK sends spans to OpenTrace with only `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT=your-opentrace-collector:4317`. 10M spans/sec load test passed and documented. All system design implementations deployed. `BENCHMARKS.md` complete for all projects.

title: 16: FULL-STACK ENGINEERING MASTERY PLAN (DATADOG ┬╖ CONFLUENT ┬╖ SNOWFLAKE)

weeks.title: Week 1: ObserveFlow тАФ Observability Collector + Ingestion + Dashboard

days.title: Monday: HTTP + OTLP Protocol + mTLS + CLI Setup
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Node.js 22, VS Code, pnpm, `curl`, `openssl`, `@opentelemetry/sdk-node` |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | HTTP/HTTPS, DNS, TLS handshake, **mTLS** (both sides present certificates тАФ used between ObserveFlow collector and backend), OTLP protocol (gRPC + HTTP/protobuf), OTel data model (trace/metric/log with shared `resource` + `attributes`) |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow raw Node.js OTLP/HTTP receiver: `POST /v1/traces`, `POST /v1/metrics`, `POST /v1/logs`. Configure a real Node.js app with `@opentelemetry/sdk-node` to send to `localhost:4318` тАФ see your own traces arrive. |

| ЁЯФЧ **Why It Matters** | This is Datadog's exact ingestion endpoint design. The `POST /v1/traces` endpoint you build today becomes the full ClickHouse pipeline in Week 9. The mTLS pattern is how the Datadog agent authenticates to the backend тАФ no API key, just a certificate. |

days.title: Tuesday: HTML + CSS + ObserveFlow Dashboard Layout
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | HTML5, CSS, Tailwind, Shadcn, Recharts |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Semantic HTML, box model, flexbox, grid, Tailwind, `cva` for metric card variants, Recharts for time-series charts |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow dashboard HTML + CSS: metrics overview cards (CPU/memory/request rate), time-series line chart (Recharts), trace waterfall skeleton (D3.js will fill it in Week 9). Lighthouse 90+. |

days.title: Wednesday: JavaScript Engine Deep
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Node.js, TypeScript, Vitest |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Primitive vs reference types, closures, event loop (call stack, microtask, macrotask), `Promise.all`/`allSettled`, generators for lazy metric pagination |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | `packages/utils/retry.ts` тАФ used by ObserveFlow collector for failed OTLP deliveries. `packages/utils/concurrent.ts` тАФ `ConcurrencyLimiter` for batching metric writes to ClickHouse. `packages/utils/emitter.ts` тАФ alert state machine. |

days.title: Thursday: TypeScript: Generics + Branded Types + Zod
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | TypeScript strict mode, Zod |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Branded types (`TraceId`, `SpanId`, `MetricName`, `ServiceName`), generics, `z.infer`, discriminated unions for OTel signal types |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | `packages/types` тАФ `TraceId`, `SpanId`, `MetricName` are branded. `packages/schemas` тАФ `SpanSchema`, `MetricPointSchema`, `LogRecordSchema` validated through Zod before any storage write. |

days.title: Friday: React + Tanstack Query + Zustand + Recharts
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | React 18, Tanstack Query, Zustand, Recharts, Nivo |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | All hooks, optimistic updates, selective subscription, `useQuery` for metrics polling, Recharts `<LineChart>` with live data |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow metrics dashboard: Recharts time-series chart updates every 10s (Tanstack Query poll). Service selector (Zustand). Sparklines on metric cards. Animated transitions on chart updates (Motion). |

days.title: Weekend: ObserveFlow Full-Stack Shell
tasks: OTLP receiver + React dashboard + PostgreSQL metadata + JWT auth. Deployed. Lighthouse 90+.

weeks.title: Week 2тАУ3: Node.js Internals + PostgreSQL + ClickHouse

days.title: Monday: V8 + Streams: High-Throughput OTLP Ingestion
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Node.js `stream/promises`, `worker_threads`, `node --inspect` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | V8 JIT, hidden classes, GC pressure, streams backpressure, `worker_threads` for CPU-bound protobuf parsing |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow OTLP stream: incoming OTLP/HTTP protobuf тЖТ Transform stream (parse protobuf in `worker_threads`) тЖТ Writable (batch to ClickHouse 500 spans at a time). 200MB log file processed in constant memory. |

| ЁЯФЧ **Why It Matters** | Datadog's backend ingests 10M+ spans/sec. Parsing protobuf synchronously would block the event loop. Moving it to `worker_threads` is the same pattern Datadog uses for CPU-intensive deserialization. |

days.title: Tuesday: ClickHouse: Metrics + Traces Storage
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | ClickHouse, Go ClickHouse client |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | MergeTree engine, partitioning by date, `ORDER BY (service, timestamp)` for fast per-service queries, sub-second aggregations on billions of rows, `EXPLAIN` on ClickHouse |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow ClickHouse schema: `spans` table (traceId, spanId, service, operation, duration, timestamp, attributes). `metrics` table (name, value, labels, timestamp). Query: `SELECT avg(duration) FROM spans WHERE service = 'payment' AND timestamp > now() - INTERVAL 1 HOUR` тЖТ < 100ms on 1B rows. |

| ЁЯФЧ **Why It Matters** | Datadog's metrics backend is ClickHouse. The same `SELECT p99(duration) FROM spans GROUP BY service` that takes 30 seconds in PostgreSQL takes 200ms in ClickHouse because of columnar storage and vectorized execution. You experience this difference yourself. |

days.title: Wednesday: Elasticsearch: Log Search
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Elasticsearch, `@elastic/elasticsearch` Node.js client |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Inverted index for full-text log search, index lifecycle management (hot тЖТ warm тЖТ cold тЖТ delete), Grok patterns for log parsing, field extraction |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow log search: `POST /v1/logs` тЖТ parse with Grok тЖТ Elasticsearch index. `GET /logs/search?query=error+payment+timeout` тЖТ full-text search results in < 50ms. Index lifecycle: logs > 30 days moved to cold storage. |

days.title: Thursday: TimescaleDB: Rollup Metrics
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | TimescaleDB, PostgreSQL |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Hypertables with automatic time partitioning, continuous aggregates (precompute hourly/daily rollups), partition pruning for time-range queries |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow TimescaleDB: raw metrics stored in ClickHouse. Hourly rollups (p50/p95/p99/avg) materialized in TimescaleDB continuous aggregate. Dashboard queries hit TimescaleDB for fast historical views. |
| ЁЯФЧ **Why It Matters** | Datadog's metrics architecture is two-tier: raw high-resolution data in a fast column store, pre-aggregated rollups for dashboard queries. You implement both. |

days.title: Friday: All 4 Isolation Levels + Indexes + N+1
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | PostgreSQL, `sqlc`, `pgxpool` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | MVCC, all 4 isolation levels with live anomaly demos, B-tree/partial/GIN indexes, `EXPLAIN ANALYZE`, N+1 elimination |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow metadata: `services`, `monitors`, `alerts`, `dashboards` in PostgreSQL. Every query `EXPLAIN ANALYZE`'d. No seq scans. `SELECT FOR UPDATE` for alert state transitions. |

weeks.title: Week 4: JWT Auth + RBAC + Redis + Webhook HMAC

days.title: Monday: RS256 JWT
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | JWT RS256, Redis, Node.js `crypto`, `AsyncLocalStorage` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | RS256 asymmetric JWT, 3-role RBAC, HMAC webhook signing, request-scoped logger via `AsyncLocalStorage` |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | `packages/auth`: JWT RS256 auth for all projects. ObserveFlow alert webhooks signed with HMAC (`X-ObserveFlow-Signature`). Request-scoped logger: every log includes `serviceId`, `traceId`, `requestId`. |

weeks.title: Week 5: Go Collector Agent + K8s DaemonSet Operator

days.title: Monday: Go Language + Concurrency
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go 1.22, `golangci-lint`, `goleak`, `go test -race` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Zero values, interfaces, error wrapping, goroutines + M:N scheduler, channels, `sync.RWMutex`, `errgroup`, `singleflight`, `atomic.Int64` |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow Go collector agent: gathers host metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network) every 10s using `gopsutil`. Reads container logs from Docker socket. Sends to ObserveFlow backend via OTLP/gRPC. Single static binary, 8MB. `go test -race` passes. `goleak.VerifyNone(t)` clean. |

| ЁЯФЧ **Why It Matters** | The Datadog agent is this exact system. It's a single Go binary that ships as a DaemonSet. It collects from the host's `cgroups`, reads Docker/containerd logs, and speaks OTLP. You build an equivalent. |

days.title: Thursday: gRPC OTLP Receiver
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | gRPC, Protocol Buffers, `buf` CLI, OTel proto definitions |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | OTLP/gRPC receiver (implement the `TraceService`, `MetricsService`, `LogsService` protobuf services), mTLS between agent and backend, streaming RPCs for log tailing |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow backend gRPC receiver: `TraceService.Export`, `MetricsService.Export`, `LogsService.Export` тАФ the same protobuf services that every OTel SDK speaks to. mTLS: agent presents cert, backend verifies. |

weeks.title: Week 6: K8s DaemonSet Operator + Alert Engine

days.title: Monday: K8s Operator Pattern
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, `controller-runtime`, Kubernetes DaemonSet |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | K8s Operator pattern, CRD (`ObserveFlowCollector`), DaemonSet management via Operator, Prometheus alert rules as CRDs |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow `CollectorOperator`: `ObserveFlowCollector` CRD тЖТ Operator deploys DaemonSet. Every new node gets an agent automatically. `kubectl apply -f collector.yaml` тЖТ agents running on all nodes in 30s. |

days.title: Thursday: Alert Engine
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Kafka, Redis, ClickHouse |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Threshold alerts, anomaly detection (mean ┬▒ 3╧Г sliding window), composite alerts (AND/OR conditions), noise reduction (don't alert on 1-minute transient spike), alert grouping + dedup |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | ObserveFlow alert engine: `(error_rate > 1%) AND (p99_latency > 500ms) FOR 5 MINUTES` тЖТ alert fires. Kafka fan-out to PagerDuty + Slack + email + WebSocket (live alert feed in dashboard). Alert dedup: same alert firing 100 times тЖТ 1 notification with count. |

days.title: Weekend: ObserveFlow COMPLETE
tasks: ObserveFlow finished. k6: 10M spans/sec ingest, ClickHouse query p99 < 200ms on 30-day data. Agent binary 8MB. DaemonSet deploys in 30s. Alert engine with noise reduction live. ADRs written. LinkedIn post. Now start VaultAuth.

weeks.title: Week 7тАУ8: VaultAuth тАФ Identity + Auth Infrastructure

days.title: Monday: OIDC Provider: Token Issuance
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, PostgreSQL, Redis, `golang-jwt/jwt` |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | OIDC discovery (`/.well-known/openid-configuration`), authorization code flow (`/auth/authorize` тЖТ redirect тЖТ `/auth/token`), ID token vs access token vs refresh token, JWKS (`/.well-known/jwks.json`), RS256 token signing |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | VaultAuth OIDC provider: full authorization code flow. ID token contains `sub`, `email`, `name`, `iat`, `exp`. Access token is short-lived (15min). Refresh token stored in Redis (7 days). JWKS endpoint serves public keys for verification. |

| ЁЯФЧ **Why It Matters** | Every company using Okta goes through this exact flow. When StreamBridge (Project 3) needs "Login with VaultAuth", it implements the authorization code flow against your OIDC endpoints тАФ the same way Confluent Cloud authenticates with Okta. |

days.title: Tuesday: SAML 2.0 Service Provider
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, `crewjam/saml` library |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | SAML 2.0 assertions, SP-initiated SSO, SAML metadata exchange, attribute mapping (SAML attributes тЖТ VaultAuth user properties) |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | VaultAuth SAML SP: enterprise customers can configure their own IdP (Google Workspace, Azure AD, Ping). SP-initiated SSO: user visits VaultAuth тЖТ redirect to enterprise IdP тЖТ SAML assertion тЖТ VaultAuth creates/updates user тЖТ issues OIDC tokens. |

days.title: Wednesday: MFA: TOTP + WebAuthn
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, `pquerna/otp` (TOTP), WebAuthn Go library |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | TOTP (RFC 6238): time-based OTP, `otpauth://` URI for QR codes, 30-second windows, drift tolerance. WebAuthn: passkeys, authenticator registration ceremony, assertion ceremony, CBOR-encoded credential |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | VaultAuth MFA: enroll TOTP (QR code тЖТ Google Authenticator). Enroll WebAuthn (passkey тЖТ Touch ID / Face ID). MFA required on login. Step-up auth: sensitive action (delete org) requires MFA re-prompt even if already logged in. |

days.title: Thursday: API Key Management
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, PostgreSQL, Redis |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | API key hashing (store only `SHA256(key)`, never the key), `last_used_at` tracking, key rotation (issue new key, old key still valid for 24h grace period), key scopes, rate limiting per key |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | VaultAuth API keys: `POST /keys` returns the key once (never again). Stored as `SHA256(key)`. `last_used_at` updated on every authenticated request. Key rotation: new key issued, old key deactivated after 24h. Rate limit: 100 req/s per key (Sorted Set in Redis). |

days.title: Friday: Audit Log: Append-Only + Tamper-Evident
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, PostgreSQL, Kafka, Elasticsearch |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Append-only table (no UPDATE/DELETE), tamper-evident hash chain (each row hashes previous row), Kafka CDC for audit log, Elasticsearch full-text search over audit events |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | VaultAuth audit log: every auth event (login, logout, MFA enroll, key created, key used, permission changed) is an immutable row. Hash chain: `hash_of_row = SHA256(previous_row_hash + row_content)`. Verify chain integrity endpoint: `GET /audit/verify`. |

weeks.title: Week 9тАУ10: StreamBridge тАФ Kafka Management + Stream Processing

days.title: Monday: Kafka Broker Architecture + KRaft
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Apache Kafka (KRaft mode), `kcat`, Go Confluent Kafka client |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | KRaft (Kafka Raft тАФ no Zookeeper), broker roles (controller vs broker vs combined), leader election within Raft quorum, ISR (In-Sync Replicas), partition assignment across brokers |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | StreamBridge: local KRaft Kafka cluster (3 nodes). Create topics, inspect partition leaders, trigger leader election (kill broker, watch leader change). StreamBridge API: `GET /clusters/{clusterId}/brokers`, `GET /clusters/{clusterId}/topics`. |

days.title: Tuesday: Schema Registry: Compatibility Enforcement
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Confluent Schema Registry, Avro, Protobuf, JSON Schema |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Schema Registry REST API, compatibility modes (BACKWARD/FORWARD/FULL/NONE), schema evolution rules (BACKWARD: new reader can read old data), Avro schema fingerprint for deduplication |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | StreamBridge Schema Registry UI: register schema, check compatibility before registering (`POST /compatibility`), view schema evolution history. Demonstrate: add nullable field (BACKWARD compatible тЬУ), remove required field (BACKWARD incompatible тАФ blocked). |

days.title: Wednesday: Consumer Group Lag Monitoring
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Kafka AdminClient, ClickHouse |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Consumer lag (log end offset тИТ committed offset), lag alerting, partition rebalancing, assignment strategies (Range, RoundRobin, Sticky) |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | StreamBridge consumer lag dashboard: poll all consumer groups every 30s via `AdminClient.ListConsumerGroupOffsets`. Store in ClickHouse (time-series). Recharts chart: consumer group lag over 24h. Alert: lag > 1000 for 5 minutes тЖТ PagerDuty via ObserveFlow (Project 1). |

days.title: Thursday: Exactly-Once Semantics
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go Confluent Kafka client, idempotent producer config |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Idempotent producer (`enable.idempotence=true`), producer epochs, sequence numbers, transactional API (`begin_transaction`, `commit_transaction`, `abort_transaction`), exactly-once semantics across topic-partition pairs |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | StreamBridge exactly-once demo: producer sends 10K messages. Kill and restart mid-way. Verify: exactly 10K messages consumed, none duplicated, none lost. Compare with non-idempotent producer (duplicates visible). |

days.title: Friday: Kafka Connect: CDC + Sink Connectors
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Kafka Connect, Debezium (CDC source), Elasticsearch sink |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Kafka Connect distributed mode, source connector (Debezium: DB changes тЖТ Kafka), sink connector (Kafka тЖТ Elasticsearch), offset management, connector REST API |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | StreamBridge: Debezium connector captures every change from VaultAuth's `audit_log` PostgreSQL table тЖТ Kafka topic тЖТ Elasticsearch sink. StreamBridge UI: manage connectors (`GET /connectors`, `POST /connectors`, `DELETE /connectors/{name}`). |

weeks.title: Week 11тАУ12: CrystalDB тАФ Columnar Query Engine + Data Sharing

days.title: Monday: Columnar Storage: Parquet + S3 Architecture
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Apache Parquet, S3, Go AWS SDK, `parquet-go` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Columnar vs row storage, Parquet row groups, metadata footer, predicate pushdown, partition pruning |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | CrystalDB storage layer: datasets stored as Parquet files on S3, partitioned by `(year, month)` directory structure. Metadata in PostgreSQL: dataset name, schema, partition list, row counts, S3 paths. |

days.title: Tuesday: ClickHouse as Query Engine
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | ClickHouse, `clickhouse-go` |

| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | External data (ClickHouse reads Parquet from S3 via S3 table engine), partition pruning (only read relevant Parquet files), result cache (same hash тЖТ return cached result), concurrent query isolation |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | CrystalDB query execution: SQL query тЖТ parse partition filter тЖТ identify relevant Parquet files on S3 тЖТ ClickHouse `SELECT` via S3 table engine тЖТ result. `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM trips WHERE month = '2024-01'` reads only January Parquet files. 100TB dataset, query returns in < 10s. |

days.title: Wednesday: Data Time-Travel
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | S3 versioning, Delta Lake (alternative approach), Go |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Time-travel queries (`AS OF TIMESTAMP` / `AS OF VERSION`), immutable snapshot files on S3, transaction log for tracking versions, rolling back to previous state |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | CrystalDB time-travel: `SELECT * FROM trips AS OF TIMESTAMP '2024-01-15 10:00:00'` returns the table as it was at that timestamp. Implemented via: every write creates a new immutable Parquet file. Transaction log tracks which files were active at each timestamp. |

days.title: Thursday: Data Sharing: Read-Only Credentials
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | AWS S3, IAM roles, Go |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Snowflake Data Sharing pattern: generate a read-only IAM role with access to specific S3 prefix, share credentials with external party, revoke by deleting IAM role |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | CrystalDB data sharing: `POST /shares` тЖТ creates read-only AWS IAM role scoped to `s3://crystaldb/{datasetId}/`. Returns temporary credentials (24h TTL). External party uses credentials to query S3 directly. Revoke: `DELETE /shares/{shareId}` тЖТ IAM role deleted. |

days.title: Friday: SQL Editor Frontend + Query History
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Next.js, React, Monaco Editor (VS Code editor in browser), Tanstack Query |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Monaco Editor integration (SQL syntax highlighting, autocomplete), streaming query results (SSE from backend), query history, query execution timeline |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | CrystalDB SQL editor: Monaco Editor with SQL syntax highlighting + column autocomplete. Submit query тЖТ SSE streams result rows as they arrive. Query history in sidebar. Execution timeline shows: parse тЖТ partition pruning тЖТ ClickHouse execution тЖТ result. |

days.title: Weekend: CrystalDB COMPLETE
tasks: All 4 Projects COMPLETE.

title: 17: FULL-STACK ENGINEERING MASTERY PLAN (JIOHOTSTAR ┬╖ RAZORPAY ┬╖ SWIGGY ┬╖ ZEPTO)

weeks.title: Week 1: PayCore тАФ Financial Ledger + Idempotent Transactions

days.title: Monday: Double-Entry Bookkeeping Logic
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, PostgreSQL, `sqlc` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Double-entry bookkeeping (assets = liabilities + equity), immutable ledger (no UPDATE on amounts), transaction atomicity, audit trails |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | PayCore ledger schema: `accounts` (id, type, balance) and `transactions` (id, from_account, to_account, amount, type, status). Transfer function: atomic debit + credit with balance check in single transaction. |

days.title: Tuesday: Idempotency Key Implementation
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Redis, PostgreSQL |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Idempotency keys, 3-tier check (Redis check тЖТ DB lock тЖТ Response cache), handling retries, `X-Idempotency-Key` header |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | PayCore idempotency layer: `POST /transfers` with `X-Idempotency-Key`. If key exists, return original response. If not, lock key in Redis, process transfer, store response, return. |

days.title: Wednesday: Webhook Delivery Engine
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, PostgreSQL, `net/http` |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Webhook architecture, HMAC signing (`SHA256`), retry with exponential backoff (1s, 10s, 1m, 10m), dead letter queue (DLQ) |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | PayCore webhook worker: process successful transfers тЖТ sign payload with HMAC тЖТ `POST` to merchant URL. If fails, schedule retry in DB. Handle `429` and `5xx` with backing off. |

weeks.title: Week 2тАУ3: DungBeetle тАФ Background Job Platform

days.title: Monday: Leader Election with Redis
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Redis |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Leader election, `SETNX` with TTL, heartbeat lease renewal, split-brain prevention, graceful failover |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | DungBeetle scheduler: 3 instances try to acquire lock `dungbeetle:leader:lock`. Only 1 wins and runs the cron loop. Heartbeat every 5s. If leader dies, another takes over in < 10s. |

days.title: Tuesday: Kafka-Backed Job Queue
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Kafka, Go |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Kafka topic-partition-offset, consumer groups, exactly-once semantics (effectively via idempotency), partition key for ordering |
| ЁЯОп **You Build** | DungBeetle queue: `POST /jobs` тЖТ Kafka topic. Multiple workers consume from group. Job types: `email`, `reconciliation`, `cleanup`. |

weeks.title: Week 4: BookWise тАФ Distributed Booking System

days.title: Monday: Distributed Locking with Fencing Tokens
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Redis, Lua |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Distributed locks, fencing tokens (monotonically increasing), Lua scripts for atomic verify-and-set |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | BookWise seat locker: `POST /seats/{id}/lock`. Acquire Redis lock with fencing token. Token passed to DB write. DB checks `WHERE fence_token > last_fence_token`. Prevents stale lock owners from writing. |

days.title: Tuesday: Saga Pattern for Payments
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Kafka, PostgreSQL |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Saga pattern (choreography), compensating transactions, eventual consistency vs finality |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | BookWise booking Saga: `Stage 1: Reserve Seat` тЖТ `Stage 2: Process Payment` (Razorpay mock) тЖТ `Stage 3: Confirm Booking`. If payment fails, trigger `Compensate: Release Seat`. |

weeks.title: Week 5: RouteMaster тАФ Marketplace Logistics

days.title: Monday: Fan-Out Notifications at Scale
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, Kafka, Redis |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Fan-out on write vs fan-out on read, push-based notifications, deduplication via Bloom filters |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | RouteMaster notification engine: 1 order update тЖТ fan-out to 3 internal topics (SMS, Push, Email). Workers handle each channel independently. Bloom filter prevents duplicate pushes within 60s. |

days.title: Tuesday: Search & Recommendation (Elasticsearch + PGVector)
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Elasticsearch, PGVector, Go |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Full-text search, inverted index, vector embeddings, cosine similarity, hybrid search |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | RouteMaster restaurant search: Elasticsearch for name/category search. PGVector for "Similar Restaurants" based on cuisine/behavior embeddings. Hybrid ranking: 70% relevance + 30% vector score. |

days.title: Weekend: All 4 Projects Integrated
tasks: PayCore handles the money, DungBeetle runs the cron, BookWise handles the slots, RouteMaster notifies the user.

weeks.title: Week 1: RipplingHR тАФ Dynamic Schemas + Permission Graph

days.title: Monday: Entity-Attribute-Value (EAV) vs JSONB Storage
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | PostgreSQL JSONB, Go |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | EAV pattern for infinite flexibility, JSONB indexing (`GIN`), JSON schema validation in DB |

| ЁЯОп **You Build** | RipplingHR: `custom_fields` table allowing any company to add "t-shirt size", "favorite snack", etc. Implement `meta` column in `employees` table using JSONB. Query to find all employees with `size=L`. |

days.title: Tuesday: ReBAC (Relationship-Based Access Control) Graph
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, SpiceDB (or similar concepts) |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Google Zanzibar model, relationships as edges, "Is employee's manager's manager allowed to see this salary?", recursive permission checks |
| ЁЯОп **You Build** | RipplingHR Auth: `relationships` table. Check: `can(user, 'read', salary_record)`. Recursive Go function to walk the management chain. |

title: 19: FULL-STACK ENGINEERING MASTERY PLAN (UBER ┬╖ DOORDASH ┬╖ PALANTIR)

weeks.title: Week 1: DashDash тАФ Real-Time Dispatch + Geofencing

days.title: Monday: H3 Hierarchical Geospatial Index
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, H3-Go |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | H3 Hexagons (Uber's choice), point-to-hex conversion, hexagon-to-multipolygon, spatial resolution |
| ЁЯОп **You Build** | DashDash: Convert driver lat/lng to H3 index (resolution 9). Find all drivers within 2 hexagons of a customer location. |

days.title: Tuesday: WebSocket Real-Time Location Updates
tasks: | ЁЯЫа **Technologies** | Go, WebSockets, Redis Pub/Sub |
| ЁЯУЦ **Concepts** | Bidirectional communication, heartbeat, connection pool, broadcast vs unicast |
| ЁЯОп **You Build** | DashDash: Driver app sends location every 2s via WS. Backend publishes to Redis topic `driver_loc:{h3_id}`. Customer app subscribes to hexagons to see moving cars. |

days.title: Weekend: DashDash Real-Time Complete
tasks: Real-time map with H3 dispatch.
